description_blurb: The FBI's 62-HQ-83894 case file includes investigative records, eyewitness testimonies, and public reports concerning Unidentified Flying Objects and flying discs documented between June 1947 and July 1968. The records include high-profile incident accounts, photographic evidence from sites like Oak Ridge, TN, and technical proposals regarding potential propulsion systems. Additional topics include convention programs, researcher accounts, and extensive media coverage from the period.  This file is partially posted on FBI vault with more redactions and some pages missing.  Included here is the complete case file with several newly declassified pages and only minor redactions.
title: 65_HS1-834228961_62-HQ-83894_Section_5
incident_location: Alexandria, Louisiana; Galveston, Texas; Los Angeles, California; southern Oregon
summary: This section of the FBI case file documents various reports and investigations concerning 'flying discs' and 'flying saucers' between 1947 and 1949. It includes an FBI investigation into a planned convention for flying saucer witnesses in Alexandria, Louisiana, and efforts to verify a sighting reported by Peter Camerlon Jones in California. The file also contains an intelligence report detailing a sighting by LTJG Joseph C. Shell of multiple elongated oval objects flying in formation in southern Oregon.
reported_object_description: Multiple objects described: 'flying saucers', 'flying disks', 'large silver metal object shaped like a child's top about the size of a balloon' with transparent portions and a greenish color; and '6 or 7 flying discs' described as 'elongated ovals, perhaps 20' in length', 'solid configuration', 'unpainted metal', possibly 'egg-shaped'.
reported_behavior: Objects reported to fly in steady file formation at 200-250 MPH with no smoke or exhaust; one object took off silently, knocking a witness to the ground; objects changed course to parallel a witness's aircraft.
official_resolution: FBI efforts to locate and identify Peter Camerlon Jones were unsuccessful, leading to the suggestion that his original letter might have been a prank. LTJG Joseph C. Shell's report was evaluated as coming from a 'reliable' source (B-3).
bust_reasoning: The bust score is high because the document itself provides strong internal evidence suggesting a mundane explanation. The FBI explicitly considers the report a 'prank' and was unable to locate the primary witness, Peter Camerlon Jones, or verify his claims. While the described behavior (silent, instantaneous takeoff, knocking witness to ground) is anomalous, its source is deemed unreliable by the investigating agency within the document.

It was stated that the convention was planned so that members may compare notes. It was also stated that flying disks were reported twice inthe week preceding the article in Alexandria, Louisiana. The above is furnished as a matter of information. cc: New Orleans [Handwritten: da] RECORDED 22 62-83894-186 F B I 34 JUL 29 1949 [Handwritten signature/initials: Ehm] FIVE EX-112 100-7545 JEJ:wjw 52 AUG 5 1949 246 # 65_HS1-834228961_62-HQ-83894_Section_5 - Page 4 Office Memorandum • UNITED STATES GOVERNMENT DATE: July 25, 1949 TO: Director, FBI FROM: SAC, Alexandria SUBJECT: SECURITY MATTER - X On July 15, 1949, the office of the Alexandria [illegible] was advised that a small flying saucer in the Alexandria (Texas) area was observed. The fact that the flying saucer was announced that it was [illegible] planning a convention for persons from all over the nation was seen. It was planned so that members [illegible] may concern [illegible] It was also based that flying disks were reported being [illegible] in Alexandria, Louisiana. The above is submitted as a matter of information. 100-4292 wlw:bal [Stamp] RECEIVED U.S. DEPT. I thought you would like to know that efforts made to locate Mr. Jones have been unsuccessful and casual inquiry in the vicinity he mentioned did not develop information about any one who is acquainted with him. I thought you might wish to consider the possibility that the original letter in this matter may have been a prank. SAC, LOS ANGELES PETTransmit the following message to: [redacted] WEST THIRTYSEVENTH STREET, LOS ANGELES, INFORMATION CONCERNING. SUBJECT ADDRESSED A LETTER TO WALTER WINCHELL RELATING THAT IN AUGUST, NINETEEN FORTYSEVEN, HE WAS HIKING IN THE MOUNTAINS OUT OF LA AND HE OBSERVED ABOUT ONE HALF BLOCK AWAY FROM HIM A LARGE SILVER METAL OBJECT SHAPED LIKE A CHILD'S TOP ABOUT THE SIZE OF A BALLOON. HE GAINED THE IMPRESSION THERE WAS LIFE WITHIN THE OBJECT THOUGH HE SAW NO ONE. HE STOOD UP AND WAVED TOWARDS THE OBJECT AND WITHIN A SECOND IT TOOK OFF, KNOCKING HIM TO THE GROUND. DISCREETLY CHECK BACKGROUND OF JONES. THEREAFTER, INTERVIEW HIM FOR THE PURPOSE OF DETERMINING ANY FACTS IN HIS POSSESSION CONCERNING THE STATEMENTS SET OUT HEREIN. Ladd [handwritten initials] SUBJECT: FLYING SAUCERS DATE: July 12, 1949 [Handwritten note in left margin: "Walter Winchell"] [Handwritten note at top right: (13)] [Right-hand column list:] Mr. Tolson Mr. Clegg Mr. Glavin Mr. Ladd Mr. Nichols [crossed out] Mr. Rosen [crossed out] Mr. Tracy Mr. Egan Mr. Gurnea Mr. Harbo Mr. Mohr Mr. Pennington Mr. Quinn Tamm Tele. Room Mr. Nease Miss Holmes Miss Gandy [Handwritten note on right: "Whitson" with checkmark] [Handwritten note on right: "motsum"] I took a call from Ernest Cuneo by reference from your office. He referred to his call of last Saturday relative to a letter Mr. Winchell received from a man in Los Angeles concerning flying saucers. He stated that Winchell wanted to do a column on this letter if the Bureau had no objection. I told him that whatever he did with reference to writing the story concerning this letter was entirely up to him and would in no way affect anything the Bureau was doing. I suggested that he might desire to check with the Air Corps inasmuch as the handling of flying saucers was a matter of primary interest to them. He stated, "To hell with the Air Force. He just didn't want to release the information if it would affect the Bureau". DML:dad [Handwritten note at bottom left: (3)] [Stamp at bottom center: 61 AUG 11 1949] RECORDED - 59 INDEXED - 59 [Handwritten red ink at bottom right: 162-83894-194] [Stamp at bottom right: 3 AUG 19 1949] [Handwritten note at bottom right: "m"] [Handwritten note at bottom right: "5"] # 65_HS1-834228961_62-HQ-83894_Section_5 - Page 20 [Faint, illegible text at top] (Right side, vertical) RECEIVED-LADD F B I U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE JUL 12 12 44 PM '49 (Center) RECEIVED-FLETCHER JUL 12 6 41 PM '49 (Center, below Fletcher) RECEIVED-H. E. KEAR '49 JUL 12 6 41 PM '49 U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE F B I DIRECT (Left side) RECEIVED JUL 13 10 54 AM '49 REC'D ESPIONAGE B I (Bottom right) RECEIVED-NICHOL JUL 12 12 12 PM '49 F B I DEPT OF JUSTICE JUL 13 3 22 PM '49 # 65_HS1-834228961_62-HQ-83894_Section_5 - Page 21 FORM NO. 64 **Office Memorandum** • UNITED STATES GOVERNMENT TO : The Director FROM : Mr. Ladd [handwritten initials] DATE: July 9, 1949 SUBJECT: FLYING SAUCERS [x] Mr. Tolson [ ] Mr. Clegg [ ] Mr. Glavin [x] Mr. Ladd [ ] Mr. Nichols [ ] Mr. Rosen [ ] Mr. Tracy [ ] Mr. Egan [ ] Mr. Gurnea [ ] Mr. Harbo [ ] Mr. Mohr [ ] Mr. Pennington [ ] Mr. Quinn Tamm [ ] Tele. Room [ ] Mr. Nease [ ] Miss Holmes [ ] Miss Gandy [Handwritten in top left margin: "cms"] Ernest Cuneo advised that Walter Winchell had received a letter from Peter Camerlon Jones, 164 West 37th Street, Los Angeles 7, California. Mr. Cuneo read the letter to me and it was very well written, obviously by a man of intelligence. In this letter Jones stated that in August of 1947 he left Los Angeles for the mountains and started hiking through the mountains. About 10:00 A. M. he was lying on the ground when he observed about one-half block away from him a large silver metal, greenish in color, shaped like a child's top and about the size of the balloons used at County Fairs. He stated that there appeared to be two windows in the object and portions of metal appeared transparent and that he gained the impression that there was some life within this object although he saw no persons. The object appeared as though [crossed out: sens] as a pressure chamber. He stood up and waved towards this object and this so-called flying saucer was off the ground in a second, knocking Jones to the ground. In its flight he stated that its power was silent and he raised the question as to whether this was an inter-global landing on our planet. He thought that it might be a device to land in our planet because the occupants of another planet had become curious as to the reaction caused by the explosion of the atomic bomb causing trouble in an expanded universe. He [handwritten: asked] left the question as to whether it was possible that the occupants of another planet might have solved the theory of negative gravity. [Handwritten in right margin: "Petiter" / "W [illegible]" / "sealed."] Mr. Cuneo stated that this letter indicated a very good knowledge of physics and that he thought it would be interesting to check into Jones' background and then possibly interview him about this alleged flying saucer. Mr. Cuneo stated that it would appear that Jones may have actually seen a flying saucer, that in any event it made an awfully good story and he requested that the Bureau keep the matter in confidence. I would recommend that the Los Angeles Office discreetly check into the background of Jones and thereafter interview him for the purpose of determining any facts he possesses about flying saucers in order that it may be determined whether his story is in any way accurate. DML:dhb [Handwritten: "Tele. EFFORTS TO IDENTIFY OR LOCATE JONES NEGATIVE. HOOD ACK PLS # 65_HS1-834228961_62-HQ-83894_Section_5 - Page 26 [Handwritten: Rec. 8/3/49] [Handwritten: Webb (Jojo.) White Bown Messburg] CONFIDENTIAL N. N. I. 96-1943 ISSUED BY THE INTELLIGENCE DIVISION OFFICE OF CHIEF OF NAVAL OPERATIONS NAVY DEPARTMENT INTELLIGENCE REPORT [Vertical text in left margin: Flying Discs] Serial: 18-49 Monograph Index Guide No.: [Blank] From: DIO-11ND at San Diego Date: 23 June 1949 Reference: CNO Conf Ser 04422P32 dtd 4 Nov 1948, subj: Flying Discs - Report Of... Source: See Below. Evaluation: B-3 Subject: Unidentified Flying Objects - Report Of... BRIEF: While flying north in a private plane source, who is believed to be reliable, saw 6 or 7 "flying discs" in southern Oregon 27 May 1949. The discs are described as elongated ovals, perhaps 20' in length; they flew in a steady file formation at between 200 to 250 MPH; they flew in a steady file formation at between 200 to 250 MPH; they appeared to be made of unpainted metal; no smoke or exhaust trails were observed. SOURCE: LTJG Joseph C. SHELL, A5(L), USNR (Inactive), Serial #348375, of 4575 Northridge Drive, Los Angeles, California. He was commissioned Ensign 5 January 1944. Served at Corpus Christi 30 days AF(T); in Dallas two months VRF-2(D). Ferried SNJ's from North American Company plants. Was at Willow Grove, Pennsylvania for 30 days. Served at check-out base for ferry squadron VRF(2) at Columbus; served at Columbus until August 1945. Served then was with VRF(3) at Terminal Island, California, where he became legal and personnel officer. Was discharged from the Navy in January 1946 as LTJG. Source holds Naval Aviator's Certificate #C-17945, plus a senior pilot's rating in the Ferry Wing of the Naval Air Transport Command. He also holds a commercial license, single and multi-engine, and flight instructor's ratings. His flight time began in 1934 and now totals over 5000 hours, of which time 1300 hours were in the U.S. Navy. Source secured a private license in 1935, a transport license in 1937, a commercial pilot's license in 1938, and a re-issue commercial pilot's license in 1945 (#184256). From March 1942 to September 1943 he was flight instructor for the Army Air Force (5 months as primary instructor at Cal-Aero, and the balance of the time as basic instructor at Lancaster, California). From September 1943 to January 1944 he flew for the Superior Oil Company of California. Since 1940 source has been Vice-President of the Royal Petroleum Company of California, Long Beach, a concern in which he and his relations own all of the stock; he also is part-owner of three other oil producing concerns in Southern California. Source is married and has three children. He has a B.S. and B.A. degree from the University of Southern California, Los Angeles, California; he also had two years of law at the University of Southern California. Source is thirty years of age, but appears to have a background of experience few men his age possess. He is believed to be conservative, sincere, absolutely reliable and very competent. OF JUSTICE [Stamp 2] RECEIVE AUG 4 4 18 PM '49 NTERNAL SECURITY F B I S DEPT OF JUSTICE # 65_HS1-834228961_62-HQ-83894_Section_5 - Page 28 CONFIDENTIAL DIO-11ND Serial 30-49 23 June 1949 Subject: Unidentified Flying Objects - Report of. REPORT: Unidentified Flying Objects Observed in Oregon 27 May 1949. On Friday, 27 May 1949, source was flying his own SNJ-type aircraft from Red Bluff, California to Burns, Oregon, a distance of 305 miles. He left Red Bluff at 1332 P.S.T. and arrived at Burns at 1458 P.S.T. (elapsed time: 1 hour 26 minutes). Source landed at Burns after circling the town twice, which consumed about ten minutes. While an SNJ's normal maximum speed is 170 MPH, source "demilitarized" his plane (which he had purchased at War Surplus, lightening the plane by about 600 lbs.) and it makes around 200 MPH true air speed. While on this flight he made 212 MPH ground speed; source estimates he had a tail wind of 15 to 18 MPH. At 1425 Pacific Standard Time, Friday, 27 May 1949, source observed the sun reflecting on an object or objects at a considerable distance ahead, a few points to the starboard. He continued to watch the course taken by the reflecting material expecting it to materialize into conventional aircraft as the distance lessened between him and the object or objects. As the objects reached the long bluffs (shown on the Boise (V-2) Aeronautical Chart as Hart Mountain) which run for a number of miles along the east side of some dry lakes (Swamp, Flagstaff, Campbell and Stone Corral Lakes) he saw that instead of a single object there were several, which seemed to be flying in formation. At this point the objects appeared to have changed their course so that they were paralleling his course (southwest to his northeast) and were following the bluffs' rim at about 1000 to 1500 feet below source's altitude, at a distance which he estimates to have been 5 1/2 to 7 1/2 miles. Source is certain that it could not have been as far as 10 miles since the bluffs were less than 10 miles away and he could see the objects outlined against the bluffs. The objects that source saw are described by him as follows: Size of each object: Considerably smaller than a fighter plane, probably less than 20' in length. All of the separate objects appeared to be the same in size. Shape of objects: There was no break in the outline. Source is certain he would have recognized conventional aircraft. They had a solid configuration, and no great thickness. They were elongated oval, perhaps twice as long as wide, and perhaps five times as long as thick. Source prints out he observed the objects from an angle from above and could not easily estimate their thickness; they could possibly have been egg-shaped, and could conceivably have been perfectly oval. The objects seemed definitely solid objects - there was nothing ethereal about them. Sp
cover_up_reasoning: There is minimal evidence of concealment. The document consists of internal FBI communications openly discussing the report and their skepticism. Redactions are routine, primarily of names and routing information, and do not obscure the core narrative or the FBI's assessment. The illegible portions appear to be due to poor document quality rather than deliberate redaction.
summary_zh: 本联邦调查局案件档案部分记录了1947年至1949年间关于“飞盘”和“飞碟”的各类报告及调查。其中包括联邦调查局对路易斯安那州亚历山大市计划召开的飞碟目击者大会的调查，以及核实彼得·卡梅隆·琼斯在加利福尼亚州报告的目击事件的努力。该档案还包含一份情报报告，详细描述了海军少尉约瑟夫·C·谢尔在俄勒冈州南部目击多个细长椭圆形物体编队飞行的事件。
reported_object_description_zh: 所报告物体描述：“飞碟”、“飞盘”；“一个大型银色金属物体，形状如儿童陀螺，大小约如气球，带有透明部分，呈绿色”；以及“6或7个飞盘”，被描述为“细长椭圆形，长度约20英尺”，“实心结构”，“未上漆金属”，可能“呈蛋形”。
reported_behavior_zh: 据报告，物体以200-250英里/小时的速度稳定编队飞行，无烟雾或排气；一个物体无声起飞，将一名目击者击倒在地；物体改变航向，与目击者的飞机平行飞行。
official_resolution_zh: 联邦调查局未能成功定位和识别彼得·卡梅隆·琼斯，导致其原始信件可能为恶作剧的推测。海军少尉约瑟夫·C·谢尔的报告被评估为来自“可靠”来源（B-3）。
bust_reasoning_zh: 驳斥度高，原因在于文件本身提供了强有力的内部证据，暗示存在世俗解释。联邦调查局明确认为该报告为“恶作剧”，并且未能找到主要目击者彼得·卡梅隆·琼斯或核实其主张。尽管所描述的行为（无声、瞬间起飞、将目击者击倒在地）异常，但其来源在文件内部已被调查机构认定为不可靠。
cover_up_reasoning_zh: 掩盖证据极少。该文件由联邦调查局内部通信组成，公开讨论了该报告及其怀疑态度。密文处理是例行性的，主要涉及姓名和路由信息，并未掩盖核心叙述或联邦调查局的评估。无法辨认的部分似乎是由于文件质量不佳而非蓄意涂改。
incident_location_zh: 路易斯安那州亚历山大市；得克萨斯州加尔维斯顿；加利福尼亚州洛杉矶；俄勒冈州南部
claims: Young Men's Business Club of Alexandria, Louisiana, planned a convention for persons who have seen flying saucers.
Flying disks were reported twice in Alexandria, Louisiana, in the week preceding July 10, 1949.
Peter Camerlon Jones claimed to have observed a large silver metal object shaped like a child's top in the mountains out of Los Angeles in August 1947, which took off, knocking him to the ground.

LTJG Joseph C. Shell, a reliable Naval Aviator, observed 6 or 7 'flying discs' described as elongated ovals, approximately 20' in length, flying in a steady file formation at 200-250 MPH in southern Oregon on May 27, 1949.
claims_zh: 路易斯安那州亚历山大市青年商业俱乐部计划为飞碟目击者举办一次大会。
1949年7月10日之前的一周内，路易斯安那州亚历山大市两次报告出现飞盘。
彼得·卡梅隆·琼斯声称于1947年8月在洛杉矶郊外山区观察到一个大型银色金属物体，形状如儿童陀螺，该物体起飞时将其击倒在地。
1949年5月27日，可靠的海军飞行员约瑟夫·C·谢尔海军少尉在俄勒冈州南部观察到6或7个“飞盘”，被描述为细长椭圆形，长度约20英尺，以200-250英里/小时的速度稳定编队飞行。
bust_explanations: Human hoax or fabricated story: The FBI's internal assessment explicitly states, 'I thought you might wish to consider the possibility that the original letter in this matter may have been a prank.' (Page 9). Furthermore, the FBI was unable to locate the witness, Peter Camerlon Jones, or verify his address (Pages 9, 15, 16), which strongly undermines the credibility of the report within the document itself.

Witness misinterpretation or misidentification: If not a deliberate hoax, the single visual observation by Jones could be a misinterpretation of a known object. The described 'knocking him to the ground' could be an exaggeration or a fall due to surprise rather than a direct physical force from the object, and 'silent' and 'instantaneous' departure could be subjective perceptions.

High-altitude balloon: The object's description as 'shaped like a child's top about the size of a balloon' (Page 17, 21) directly suggests a balloon-like object. The 'silver metal' and 'greenish in color' could be reflections or specific materials, and 'transparent portions' or 'two windows' could be features of a complex balloon or scientific instrument package. The 'silent' aspect is consistent with a balloon.
bust_explanations_zh: 人为恶作剧或捏造故事：联邦调查局的内部评估明确指出，“我认为您可能希望考虑此事的原始信件可能是一场恶作剧的可能性。”（第9页）。此外，联邦调查局未能找到目击者彼得·卡梅隆·琼斯或核实其地址（第9、15、16页），这在文件本身内部严重削弱了报告的可信度。
目击者误解或误认：如果不是蓄意恶作剧，琼斯的单一视觉观察可能是对已知物体的误解。“将其击倒在地”的描述可能是一种夸大，或是因惊吓而跌倒，而非物体直接施加的物理力；而“无声”和“瞬间”离开可能是主观感知。
高空气球：物体被描述为“形状如儿童陀螺，大小约如气球”（第17、21页），直接暗示其为气球状物体。“银色金属”和“绿色”可能是反射或特定材料，而“透明部分”或“两个窗户”可能是复杂气球或科学仪器包的特征。“无声”的特点与气球一致。
